title: elaeocarpus ferrugineus subsp. glabrescens
intro: Elaeocarpus ferrugineus subsp. glabrescens, also known as the 'Ironwood' or 'Kapok Tree', is a plant species native to tropical regions, characterized by its distinctive iron-colored wood and small, white, fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus ferrugineus subsp. glabrescens include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
